description: A plain force-push to a shared branch overwrites commits a teammate pushed in the meantime, deleting their work from the remote.
body: Failure mode: a rebase or amend is followed by git push --force, which replaces the remote tip with the local one and discards any commits pushed by others since the last fetch. Detection signals: a teammate reports their commit vanished; git reflog on the remote or CI shows a tip that no longer contains known commits; the push reported no rejection despite the remote having advanced. Do not use plain --force on any shared branch. Safer fallback: fetch first and push with --force-with-lease so the push aborts when the remote moved, and coordinate any intentional rewrite with the team.
